ticket.raw_subject: Meeting Request: DHL Express & Dokan Integration via Google Meet
ticket.description:
Hello PluginHive Team,

I am developing a WooCommerce marketplace using Dokan (Free Version) and I am interested in your PH DHL Shipping Plugin. I was looking at your $319 (Popular) plan and I need to confirm if it suits my needs.

I have two main requirements:

Live Rates calculated from each vendor's origin address.
Vendors being able to generate and download shipping labels directly from their Dokan dashboard.
I would like to schedule a short meeting via Google Meet to clarify if this plugin supports this workflow and if it is compatible with Dokan Free.

Please let me know your availability.

Best regards,

Diogo Santos Europlazza Lda

comments.body:
Hello Diogo,

Thank you for the detailed overview of your requirements — we can address both of them.

WSS (**WooCommerce Shipping Services**) is the PluginHive plugin that includes DHL Express along with 20+ other carriers, and it does support Dokan as one of its primary multivendor integrations. Here is how your two requirements are covered:

1. Live rates from each vendor's origin address: Yes, WSS is built to calculate rates using each vendor's ship-from address in a multivendor setup. At checkout, rates reflect the correct origin location per vendor, so customers see accurate shipping costs across mixed-vendor carts.

2. Vendor label generation from the Dokan dashboard: Yes, vendors can generate and download labels directly from their Dokan vendor panel. WSS embeds its shipping interface within the Dokan vendor dashboard, so vendors can manage their own fulfillment without needing WooCommerce admin access.

Two points worth clarifying with our team before you commit:

- Dokan Free compatibility: Our integration is confirmed for Dokan broadly, but we want to verify that the vendor dashboard features your workflow depends on are available specifically in the Free version (versus Dokan Pro). Our team can confirm this during the meeting.

- Plan for multivendor features: Multivendor functionality in WSS is tied to a specific plan tier. We'd recommend confirming on the call whether the $319 Popular plan covers your full setup — vendor count, monthly shipment volume, and DHL Express access — or whether a higher tier applies.

We will be happy to assist you with the plugin setup over a Zoom/Google Meet call. Please install the plugin and add your shipping carrier accounts and let us know. We will setup a time to connect and assist you further.

Thanks and regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

Hello Diogo,

Great to hear you have connected your DHL Express account — that is a key step completed.

Regarding the three points DHL raised in their email, here are the answers:

1. Complete customs data entry
Yes, the plugin supports all the customs fields DHL mentioned. For each product, you can set the HS code, Country of Origin (country of manufacture), customs description, and declared value. Item weight and dimensions are also captured per product and included in the shipment request. These fields are configured under the plugin's Products section. When you generate an international DHL Express label, all of this customs data is automatically sent to DHL via their API.

2. Commercial invoice
Yes, the plugin sends commercial invoice data as part of the DHL label request for international shipments. There is also a specific option to include shipping charges on the commercial invoice, which you can enable under Settings > Carriers > DHL Express > Other Details.

3. Sandbox/test environment
Yes, the plugin supports both test (sandbox) and production modes for DHL Express. DHL provides separate credentials for each environment through their Developer Portal at developer.dhl.com. You can configure the mode in the plugin under Settings > Carriers > DHL Express. Once you are ready to go live, switching to production credentials is straightforward.

Regarding DHL's note about PluginHive not being a "certified" solution — this is typical wording DHL uses for third-party integrations. The plugin connects via DHL's official API, so all the standard DHL features and data requirements are fully supported.

Once you have had a chance to set up the product customs fields, we can schedule our follow-up call to complete the full multi-vendor setup. You can use the same booking link Touseef shared to pick a time that works for you.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive

Hello Diogo,

Adding to my previous response.

Regarding the three points DHL raised in their email, here are the answers:
1. Complete customs data entry
Yes, the plugin supports all the customs fields DHL mentioned. For each product, you can set the HS code, Country of Origin (country of manufacture), customs description, and declared value. Item weight and dimensions are also captured per product and included in the shipment request. These fields are configured under the plugin's Products section. When you generate an international DHL Express label, all of this customs data is automatically sent to DHL via their API.
FYI, you can update your products with customs information within the plugin >products page

2. Commercial invoice
Yes, the plugin sends commercial invoice data as part of the DHL label request for international shipments. The plugin also sends custom documents to DHL electronically to elimate paper work, which you can enable under Settings > Carriers > DHL Express > Other Details.

3. Sandbox/test environment
Yes, the plugin supports both test (sandbox) and production modes for DHL Express. DHL provides separate credentials for each environment through their Developer Portal at developer.dhl.com. You can configure the mode in the plugin under Settings > Carriers > DHL Express. Once you are ready to go live, switching to production credentials is straightforward.


Feel free to reachout to us anytime you need assistance from our end.

Thanks & Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

Hello Diogo,

Looking at the screenshot you shared, the issue is clear — the Account Number field is empty and highlighted in red. This field is required and is separate from the API Username and Password.

Your DHL Account Number is your DHL Express shipping account number (typically a 9-digit number). This would have been assigned to you when your DHL Express business account was originally created — it is not the same as the API credentials you just received.

To find it, you can:
1. Check the original DHL Express account confirmation email you received when signing up.
2. Log in to the DHL Business portal and look under your account profile.
3. Contact your DHL account manager — since you are already in touch with their team, they can confirm the number immediately.

Once you have the Account Number, enter it in that field and click Connect. That should complete the setup.

If you run into any further issues after entering the account number, please let us know and we can arrange another short call to complete the configuration.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive

Hello Diogo,

Good progress — you have found the correct credentials from the DHL Developer Portal.

However, looking at the screenshot you shared earlier today, there is one more step needed before the plugin can connect for your live store. Your DHL app is currently approved for the sandbox testing environment only (shown as "Customer (Integration) Testing" in the portal). To use DHL Express on your live store, you need to request production access:

1. Log in to the DHL Developer Portal at developer.dhl.com
2. Open your app (DHL Express - MyDHL API - Europlazza Lda. - PT)
3. Click the "Request Production Access" button shown at the bottom of the APIs section
4. DHL will review and approve your production application — this typically takes a few business days

Once DHL approves production access, they will provide you with production credentials. You should then enter those production credentials in the plugin under Settings > Carriers > DHL Express, making sure the mode is set to production (not test/sandbox).

In the meantime, if you want to verify the plugin connection works before production is approved, you can enter your current credentials and set the plugin to test mode — just note that test mode cannot generate real labels or live rates.

One important security note: since your API password was shared in this support ticket, we strongly recommend using the "Change password" button visible in your DHL Developer Portal to generate a new API password once you are done testing. This keeps your account secure.

When your production credentials are ready, please feel free to schedule another call and our team will help you complete the full setup including the multi-vendor configuration.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive

Hello Diogo,

Great to hear everything is working well!

When you receive your production DHL Express credentials, you will not need to set up the plugin from scratch. You only need to update the existing DHL Express carrier configuration with a few quick changes:

1. Go to the WSS app dashboard and open the DHL Express carrier settings.
2. Replace the current Site ID and Site Password with your production credentials.
3. Check the "Production Key" checkbox — this is currently unchecked since the account is in test mode.
4. Save the settings and reconnect the carrier.

All your other configurations — vendor sync, packaging rules, services, rate automation, and the Dokan multi-vendor setup — will remain exactly as they are. Only the credentials and the production mode toggle need to be updated.

Once done, we recommend placing a quick test order to confirm that rates and labels are generating correctly with the live account.

If you run into anything during the switch, feel free to reach out and we will assist you.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive

comments.plain_body: Hello,     I´ll have multiple vendors in my website and from different countries. They´ll need different DHL accounts from their origin countries or can I just use mine?     If so, how do I do it with the plugin?     Best regards,  Diogo Santos

comments.body:
Hello Diogo,

Good Day!

Since your vendors are based in different countries, each vendor will generally need their own DHL Express account. DHL account numbers are tied to specific account holders and country of origin, so a single account from your country would not cover shipments generated from a vendor located in, say, Germany or Brazil.

The good news is that WSS does support this setup with Dokan. In the plugin, each vendor's DHL Express account can be configured with their own carrier credentials and ship-from address, so their rates and labels are generated using their own DHL account.

Let us know for any further queries.

Best Regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive
